entry.title: Unable to Deploy Joules Skills in SAP BPA - ClientNotFound
entry.content:
Hello, I have an error when I try to deploy or test Joule in SAP BPA.

"The project cannot be deployed by the component "Integration Gateway". Error while trying to connect to Joule - JouleClientService - invalid_client - Client Not Found"

I have the roles for das-application but it still doesn't reaches. Also, when I try to test, the environments don't show up. 

Here some examples:

entry.title: MCP Server Tool Test Fails in Joule Agent with 6010 Error
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am facing an issue while testing an MCP server tool in the Joule Agent and would appreciate your guidance.

Scenario

I have implemented an MCP server using FastMCP with a custom tool that executes a SPARQL query against SAP HANA Knowledge Graph. The server runs in streamable-http mode.

Observations

  • The MCP server starts successfully.

  • All tools are listed correctly when the MCP server is registered.
  • The tool metadata is visible and selectable in the Joule Agent.
  • However, when I configure the Destination and attempt to test the tool, the test fails with Error 6010.

Key Point

The same MCP server and tool work correctly when tested locally and are properly displayed in the MCP server. The failure only occurs during destination-based testing in the Joule Agent.

Environment

  • MCP Framework: FastMCP

  • Transport: streamable-http

  • Backend: SAP HANA Cloud (Knowledge Graph / SPARQL)

  • Authentication: Destination-based

  • Error: 6010 during tool test

Question

  • What does error 6010 indicate in the context of Joule Agent MCP destination testing?

  • Are there any specific requirements for:

    • MCP endpoint structure

    • Response format

    • Authentication headers

    • Health or metadata endpoints

  • Is additional configuration required for FastMCP–based servers to be compatible with Joule Agent destinations?

Any guidance or documentation references would be very helpful.

sap-joule-studio-mcp-server:true property added in destination
"
import os
import json
import logging
from typing import Dict, Any

from hdbcli import dbapi
from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)

# --------------------------------------------------
# MCP Server
# --------------------------------------------------
mcp = FastMCP(
    name="sap-hana-kg-mcp",
    host="0.0.0.0",
    port=int(os.getenv("PORT", 8080)),
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# HANA Connection via VCAP_SERVICES
# --------------------------------------------------
def get_hana_connection():
    #vcap = json.loads(os.environ["VCAP_SERVICES"])
    #hana = vcap["hana"][0]["credentials"]

    return dbapi.connect(
        user="DBADMIN",
        password="password@2103",
        address="address",
        port=443,
        encrypt="true",
        sslValidateCertificate="true",
    )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Utility: JSON-safe rows
# --------------------------------------------------
def serialize_rows(rows😞
    return [
        [str(col) if col is not None else None for col in row]
        for row in rows
    ]

# --------------------------------------------------
# MCP Tool
# --------------------------------------------------
@mcp.tool(
    description="Execute a SPARQL query against SAP HANA Knowledge Graph and return results"
)
def execute_sparql(query: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Input:
      - query: SPARQL query string

    Output:
      - status, row_count, results
    """
    try:
        GRAPH_URI = "http://new_test_mission_faqhanahotspots.org/"

        conn = get_hana_connection()
        cursor = conn.cursor()

        cursor.callproc(
            "SPARQL_EXECUTE",
            (
                query,
                "Query from Joule MCP",
                GRAPH_URI,
                None,
            ),
        )

        rows = cursor.fetchall()

        cursor.close()
        conn.close()

        return {
            "status": "success",
            "row_count": len(rows),
            "results": serialize_rows(rows[:10]),
        }

    except Exception as e:
        import traceback
        return {
            "status": "error",
            "message": str(e),
            "trace": traceback.format_exc(),
        }



# --------------------------------------------------
# Run MCP
# --------------------------------------------------
if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run(
        transport="streamable-http" )
"

 

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: I need a POV using Joule via BTP, but keeping data and execution on-prem or in NS2.
entry.content:
I need a POV using Joule via BTP, but keeping data and execution on-prem or in NS2.  Here is my answer, wanted review of my pov.

POV

1. Architecture Approach

  • Hybrid Model:
    • Joule UI & orchestration via SAP BTP (public cloud).
    • Data & execution remain on-prem or NS2 using:
      • SAP AI Core/AI Foundation deployed in NS2 or on-prem.
      • Secure connectors (SAP Cloud Connector, Private Link) for controlled interaction.
  • Inference Strategy:
    • Joule prompts processed in BTP but routed to local AI runtime for execution.
    • Sensitive data never leaves NS2/on-prem; only metadata or anonymized context flows to BTP.

2. Key Benefits

  • Compliance & Sovereignty:
    • Meets FedRAMP, ITAR, and other regulatory requirements.
  • Performance & Latency:
    • Local execution reduces dependency on public cloud for heavy workloads.
  • Innovation Access:
    • Still leverages Joule’s capabilities without compromising security.

3. Challenges

  • Integration Complexity:
    • Requires robust connectivity and governance between BTP and NS2/on-prem.
  • Feature Parity:
    • Some Joule features may depend on public cloud AI services; need fallback or custom models locally.
  • Cost & Maintenance:
    • Running AI Core locally adds infrastructure overhead.

4. Recommended Actions

  • Assess Use Cases:
    • Identify which Joule scenarios require sensitive data handling.
  • Deploy AI Core in NS2:
    • Use SAP AI Core/AI Foundation for local inference.
  • Implement Secure Connectivity:
    • SAP Cloud Connector + Private Link for controlled communication.
  • Governance Framework:
    • Define data classification, prompt filtering, and audit mechanisms.

Key Components

  1. Joule Interface
    Embedded in SAP applications (e.g., S/4HANA, SuccessFactors) for user interaction.

  2. SAP BTP (Public Cloud)
    Acts as the orchestration layer for Joule, handling prompt routing and metadata exchange.

  3. Secure Connectivity

    • SAP Cloud Connector and Private Link ensure encrypted, controlled communication between BTP and NS2/on-prem systems.

  4. On-Prem/NS2 AI Runtime

    • SAP AI Core/AI Foundation deployed locally for inference and processing.
    • Sensitive data never leaves the secure environment.

  5. ERP Systems (S/4HANA, ECC)
    Hosted in NS2/on-prem for transactional and operational data.

  6. Compliance & Governance Layer

    • Enforces FedRAMP, ITAR, and other regulatory standards.
    • Includes audit, logging, and prompt filtering.

Data Flow

  • User → Joule → BTP → Secure Connector → NS2 AI Core → ERP Systems
  • Only metadata or anonymized context flows to BTP; all sensitive data and execution remain local.

Benefits

  • Regulatory Compliance: Meets strict security and sovereignty requirements.
  • Innovation Access: Leverages Joule without compromising sensitive data.
  • Performance: Local execution reduces latency for critical workloads.

entry.title: SAP Joule Integration with SuccessFactors (Custom & Standard Portlet Support)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently in the final phase of implementing SAP Joule with SAP SuccessFactors and are preparing for an upcoming go-live. During our testing and validation cycles, we have identified critical limitations that are impacting our Joule use cases and overall readiness.

We are submitting this as a High Priority Enhancement Request, as the issues directly affect business adoption and scope finalization.

Issue 1: Lack of Support for Custom Portlets / Custom MDF Objects

Description:
Our SuccessFactors landscape includes several custom portlets and custom MDF objects that store critical employee and business data. Currently, SAP Joule is unable to retrieve, interpret, or respond with information from these custom portlets.

We have reviewed the official Joule implementation and configuration guides; however, we did not find any documented configuration, extensibility option, or workaround that allows Joule to consume data from custom portlets or MDF objects.

Business Impact:

  • Limits Joule’s usability to only a subset of standard data
  • Prevents adoption of Joule for organization-specific use cases
  • Reduces ROI on Joule implementation
  • Forces continued manual navigation and reporting for custom data

Request / Enhancement Ask:

  1. Please confirm whether SAP Joule currently supports custom portlets or custom MDF objects.
  2. If supported:
    • Kindly share the required configuration steps, permissions, APIs, or extensibility options.
  3. If not supported:
    • Please advise on the product roadmap for custom object support.
    • Suggest any recommended alternative approach to expose custom data for Joule-based use cases.

Issue 2: Joule Not Working for Certain Standard SuccessFactors Portlets

Description:
We have also identified that Joule is not functioning as expected for the following standard SuccessFactors portlets:

  • Bank Information
  • Dependent Information

Despite these being standard delivered portlets, Joule does not return, process, or interact with data from these areas during testing.

Business Impact:

  • Incomplete employee self-service experience
  • Limits Joule’s effectiveness for HR and employee queries
  • Creates inconsistencies across supported and unsupported data domains

Request / Enhancement Ask:

  1. Please validate whether Bank Information and Dependent Information portlets are officially supported by Joule.
  2. Share any required prerequisites, role-based permissions, or configuration settings needed to enable Joule functionality for these portlets.
  3. Confirm whether this behavior is:
    • A known issue
    • A documented product limitation
    • Planned for enhancement in upcoming releases

Priority & Go-Live Impact

Given our upcoming go-live, clarity on the above items is critical for:

  • Finalizing Joule use cases
  • Managing business expectations
  • Determining scope and adoption readiness

We would appreciate your guidance, confirmation, and any available documentation or roadmap insights at the earliest.

Thank you for your continued support. We look forward to your response.

entry.title: SAP Joule Agent with SAP oData MCP Issue
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are trying to create an SAP Joule Agent with MCP

We have followed steps for developing the same using following blog on SAP oData mcp

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-odata-to-mcp-server-running-on-btp/ba-p/14220561

  1. Accordingly, we have imported the project from GitHub
  2. We have configured destinations accordingly for SAP on premise S/4HANA system
  3. Built and deployed the MCP successfully on BTP Subaccount
  4. We have also created a destination in subaccount to access above mcp server

   https://help.sap.com/docs/Joule_Studio/45f9d2b8914b4f0ba731570ff9a85313/3d9dfad0bc39468292d508f0808a12fe.html

 

  1. We have created SAP joule agent accordingly
  2. And trying to add / configure the mcp server
  3. However, while adding its showing below mentioned error after selecting the destinations -

Unable to find MCP server tools for destination

Unable to find MCP server tools for destination XXXXXXXXXXX. The destination tried to provide authorization tokens but failed in all cases. This is most likely due to misconfiguration. Original error messages: Bad credentials

 

  1. We tried all the possible types in destination service eg. OAuth2Password, OAuth2ClientCredentials , OAuth2JWTBearer , OAuth2TokenExchange but still we are getting the error relevant to authorization
  2. How to resolve this issue, what kind of destination authentication we should use for this?

Regards,

 

Nilesh Rane

entry.title: Issues with Joule AI on SAP SuccessFactors Test Instance & Customization of AI Instructions
entry.content:
Issues with Joule AI behavior and customization options

We have tested several times the Joule  Joule AI implemented on our SAP SuccessFactor test instance, and we are repeatedly facing error messages such as:

  • “I’m sorry, but I can’t…”

  • "I am unable to ..."
  • “This action is not available at the moment”

(see screenshot attached below)

Could you please explain why these messages appear? We would like to understand whether this is related to system configuration, authorizations, current limitations of Joule, or the test environment.

In addition, we would like to know if it is possible to customize Joule’s instructions or responses.
For example, when asking “How can I create a job requisition?”, Joule provides the standard SAP SuccessFactors procedure, whereas we would like it to return our internal company process instead.

Is it possible to modify or configure Joule so that it uses company-specific procedures or documentation rather than the default SAP SuccessFactors guidance?

Finally, could you please let us know how to formally submit or escalate enhancement / evolution requests related to Joule AI (e.g. new functionalities, customization capabilities, or improvements)?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: “Why didn’t my Joule Agent call MCP get_supplier_information method even though the tool is register
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


I am working with SAP BTP Joule Agent and have integrated an external MCP Server to retrieve supplier master data and MCP Server is successfully added.

*Agent Details & Instructions 
Expertise : You are an expert in inventory analysis and supplier management, providing actionable business recommendations based on product, order, and supplier data.

**Purpose & Objectives**
The Inventory Intelligent Agent assists users in optimizing inventory and business planning by leveraging supplier information and analyzing product and order data.
- Identify and explain inventory status and supplier information for individual products.
- Deliver clear, actionable business recommendations, such as stock adjustments or supplier changes, to optimize inventory and procurement outcomes.
- Ensure all outputs are structured, explainable, and reference specific orders or suppliers to justify recommendations.

**Process**
1. Use available tools to retrieve product list, order details, and supplier information.
2. Analyze product properties (e.g., price, stock, supplier, units on order) and order history for each product.
3. Identify opportunities for inventory optimization, such as adjusting stock levels or considering alternative suppliers.
4. Provide clear, numeric business recommendations (e.g., "increase stock of Product X by 20 units" or "Consider switching to Supplier Y for Product Z"), citing specific orders, product data, and supplier details as justification.
5. Structure the output to include: (a) the inventory status or supplier recommendation, (b) the supporting data and cited orders or suppliers, and (c) the recommended action with rationale.
6. If data is missing or unclear, ask the user for clarification rather than making unsupported assumptions.

**Tooling**
The Inventory Intelligent Agent can access the following tools:
- **Get Order Details per Order** – Retrieve order details for a specific order.
- **Get Order Details for Products** – Retrieve all order details associated with a specific product.
- **Get Products** – Retrieve product information including supplier details and inventory status.

**Supplier Information**
Use MCP Server available tools to retrieve supplier information.
- **Supplier Information** – Retrieve detailed supplier information to support procurement and inventory decisions.

MCP Details

Tested in MCP Inspector

MCP method get_supplier_information is registered and visible in Joule
Expected Behavior

When I ask a question like:

“Who is the supplier of product Chai?”

The Joule Agent should call the MCP method get_supplier_information and return supplier details.
and it worked once but not working now!
Instead of calling the MCP tool, Joule responds with a fallback message like:

“The supplier for the product ‘Chai’ is not included in the provided data. Could you provide more details?”

My Questions

How can I force Joule Agent to always invoke an MCP tool when supplier information is requested?

Are there best practices for agent instructions to avoid fallback LLM answers?

Does mixing analytics + supplier retrieval in a single agent reduce MCP tool invocation confidence?

Are there examples or guidance for Vibe coding Joule agents with mandatory MCP usage?

Any guidance, documentation, or examples would be highly appreciated.

Tool

"""
Supplier-related tool handlers for the MCP Supplier server.
This module contains only supplier-specific tool implementations.
"""


import json
import logging
from collections.abc import Sequence
from mcp.types import Tool, TextContent
from .toolhandler import ToolHandler

logger = logging.getLogger("mcp-supplier")


class GetSupplierDetailsToolHandler(ToolHandler😞
    """
    Tool handler for getting supplier detail for a product.
    """

    def __init__(self😞
        super().__init__("get_supplier_details")

    def get_tool_description(self) -> Tool:
        """
        Return the tool description for supplier detail lookup.
        """
        return Tool(
            name=self.name,
            description="""
            Get supplier detail for a specified supplierId.
            It extracts the supplierId from product information, maps the supplierId code to a human-readable description, and returns a formatted summary.
            """,
            inputSchema={
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "supplierId": {
                        "type": "integer",
                        "description": "Unique identifier of the supplier to fetch Supplier detail for, if the parameter supplierId isn't in number please convert to Number before invoking this function."
                    }
                },
                "required": ["supplierId"]
            }
        )

    async def run_tool(self, args: dict) -> Sequence[TextContent]:
        try:
            self.validate_required_args(args, ["supplierId"])

            supplierId = args["supplierId"]
            logger.info(f"Fetching supplier details for product:")

            # ---- Internal resolution (NO LLM CHAINING) ----
           
            supplier_data = {
                    "SupplierID": supplierId,
                    "SupplierName": "Exotic Liquids",
                    "ContactPerson": "Charlotte Cooper",
                    "Title": "Purchasing Manager",
                    "Address": "49 Gilbert St., London",
                    "Country": "UK",
                    "Phone": "(171) 555-2222",
                    "Fax": "(171) 555-2223",
                    "Email": "contact@exoticliquids.example",
                    "LeadTimeDays": 5,
                    "MinOrderQty": 50,
                    "Status": "De-Active"
                }
               
            return [
                TextContent(
                    type="text",
                    text=json.dumps(supplier_data, indent=2)
                )
            ]

        except Exception as e:
            logger.exception("Supplier lookup failed")
            return [
                TextContent(
                    type="text",
                    text=json.dumps({"error": str(e)}, indent=2)
                )
            ]

entry.title: SAP Joule activation in Concur
entry.content:
We are planning to activate joule in SAP concur and need clarification on below.

currently we have already enabled Concur SSO with corporate identity(Concur -> Corporate IDP) and joule activation required authentication from joule ->SAP IAS -> corporate IDP.

If this authentication is enabled, will it have any impact on the existing SSO configuration with the corporate IdP?

Also confirm if both authentications work parallelly or need to reconfigure existing authentication via concur (joule) -> SAP IAS-> corporate IDP to enable joule?

 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Tutorial - Use Joule Studio to Create an Agent
entry.content:
Hi All,

Following the tutorial "Use Joule Studio to Create an Agent" (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/joulestudio-agent-create.html), I have an errore in the test phase:

Debug session failed (ID: 87b6c9fd-e66b-4d3d-a980-e94dd4010df6)

  • Unable to deploy tested project in environment (id: privatejclj)
  • The project (ID: eu10.build-tests-04.maintenanceorderskills, version: 1.0.1-SNAPSHOT.1769511533) in the environment (ID: privatejclj) could not be deployed.
  • An error occurred while deploying an artifact to a runtime. (component: Integration Gateway)
  • Deployment cannot be performed. Agent Builder is not enabled.

Data: 27/01/2026, 11:59:11

Does anyone has faced the same issue?

@martin_plummer Tagging you since you are the developer (I hope it is not a problem).

Thanks,

Samuele

entry.title: SAP JOULE with S/4HANA ON-PREMISE systems
entry.content:
Hello experts 

We are working in a SAP S/4HANA implementation, and the version will be 2025, but it will in on-premise architecture. 

Ok, I checked and the customer needs a license to implement, in the SAP BTP. Additionally, there is a SAP NOTE https://me.sap.com/notes/3632703/E  that says the Joule is not possible install using a SAP on-premise solutions. 

My point is, why? 
If I have a Global Subscription in BTP, and with the SAP AI Core, including the SAP Integration Suite getting communication with my SAP S/4HANA on-premise, this SAP Note (3632703) is correct? 
Because I believe in the architecture and technical view, it's totally possible. Please could you help me to understand this point?

Thank you

entry.title: how to use Joule during different phases such as Dev, QA, PRD if I only have 1 unit in global quota
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

If I only have 1 unit for Joule in BTP global quota, how can I use Joule during different phases such as Dev, QA, prod?

Any tips or insights would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance!

Joule

entry.title: Static URL of the Joule application
entry.content:
As part of activating the Joule plugin in our S/4HANA PCE (on-premise) Fiori LaunchPad I have created a Fiori catalog including a SAPUI5 Fiori App (plugin for Joule) as described in the following SAP Help portal documentation; Configure and Activate Joule Plug-In in a Target Mapping | SAP Help Portal.

The above solution design does not seem to match the fact that we have a Joule subscription/application in each of our four BTP subaccounts that are connected to our four respective S/4 HANA PCE (on-premise) systems (sandbox, development, test, production).

Because, this again means that we have four different URL's for this Joule plugin app:

https://<sandbox subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4
https://<development subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4
https://<test subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4
https://<production subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4

So, finally, this will result in having system specific Fiori catalogs and related roles?!

Is there any way around this?

entry.title: How much time/effort customer need to take to activate Joule on SuccessFactors EC?
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

SuccessFactors customer with EC module currently is interested in SAP Joule feature. 

But they concern how much time/effort it may take them to activate Joule on SF EC. 

Is there any benchmark data or similar user case we can refer to?

Thanks,

Andy (SAP CSM)

entry.title: Enable Joule in SAP BTP Cockpit
entry.content:
Hello all,
I am using a trial account of SAP BTP.

As I'm progressing on SAP Discovery Center Mission, I'm struggling with enabling Joule in SAP BTP Cockpit - the mission being:


From what I gathered, this could be the explanation for not being able to accomplish this:
"Before you can see the Joule icon in the cockpit tool bar, a global account administrator first needs to enable Joule. By navigating to the Global Account Settings and selecting Joule, an administrator accepts the terms and conditions and toggles the button Enable Joule. Now all account users will be able to see the Joule icon and get started with Joule."

Does anyone know what could solve this enablement?
Thank you in advance!

entry.title: BPA Unable to verify the signature of the archive (Transport Joule Skills)
entry.content:
Hi guys, could anyone help me out?

In SAP Build Process Automation (BPA), I’m trying to migrate/transport a Joule Skill project from Subaccount A (US, Azure) to Target Subaccount B (another region, US VA, AWS).

The import fails with this error: 

Unable to verify the signature of the archive. Make sure the landscape keys from landscape us21-prod are imported in your landscape, if not, contact DevOps. Ensure that the other potential reasons for this error are checked. This error could happen for the following reasons: Couldn't get key xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx version from credential store.

The same error - Transport and Upload proyect.

 

I’ve searched everywhere and couldn’t find anything useful.

I did find an SAP Note that suggests using a tenant in the same region, which is honestly very limiting because it would force us to change our entire landscape design just because of BPA:

 

 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3472593/E

Has anyone found a way to solve this (e.g., importing landscape keys / fixing credential store key access) and successfully transport BPA/Joule projects across regions?

I already opened a ticket with SAP, but I haven’t received an answer yet.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Can a SAP Build Action be setup to stream output to a SAP Joule Skill?
entry.content:
We have an API which streams the AI Reasoning output in real-time.

We can surface the streamed output at a command line by calling our API as follows:

 try:
        with requests.post(
            API_URL,
            json=payload,
            headers=headers,
            stream=True,  # 👈 enables streaming
        ) as response:
        
Ideally, we'd like to present this streamed output in a SAP Joule Skill, with an action for our API. However,  the output is not streamed,  it returns only the final step of the output in one go, so not only is the full response not shown, it also takes longer to appear, which is not a good user experience.

Can Joule Skills/SAP Build Actions handle streamed output and how that acheived?

entry.title: How to leverage the custom Joule skill in S4HANA PCE Fiori launchpad?
entry.content: We have developed a custom Joule skill using SAP Build, and it is returning the expected response. Joule has also been enabled in the S/4HANA 2025 PCE Fiori Launchpad. How can we consume or leverage the response from this custom Joule skill within the Fiori Launchpad?

entry.title: Leveraging Joule Custom Skills in S/4HANA PCE: A Clean Core Approach for Sourcing & Procurement
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

As a consultant focused on RISE with SAP and Clean Core strategies, I’ve been exploring how Joule (SAP’s Generative AI) can transform the user experience within S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (PCE), specifically for Sourcing & Procurement.

Integrating a custom skill developed in SAP Build with the Fiori Launchpad (S/4HANA 2025) is more than just a technical milestone; it is a strategic shift. For those of us managing large-scale environments, this represents a powerful way to handle extensibility Side-by-Side on SAP BTP, keeping the core stable and upgrade-ready.

Key Insights from my recent implementation:

  1. Enhancing Procurement Efficiency: By leveraging custom skills, we can empower users to query complex contract details or PR/PO statuses through natural language, significantly reducing the volume of L1/L2 support tickets.

  2. Clean Core Alignment: Using SAP Build Actions to consume OData APIs ensures that we are following SAP Best Practices, avoiding legacy "Z" developments inside the S/4HANA core.

  3. The SAP Activate Connection: During the Run phase, Joule acts as a continuous enabler, helping users navigate business processes without leaving the Fiori environment.

I’d love to hear your thoughts: For those already working with Joule in S/4HANA 2025 PCE, how are you handling the governance of these custom skills? Are you prioritizing "Read-only" informational skills, or are you moving towards transactional "Write" scenarios (e.g., creating Purchase Requisitions) in production?

Looking forward to exchanging experiences on this AI journey!

#RISEwithSAP #S4HANA #SAPMM #CleanCore #Joule #SAPBuild #BTP #SourcingAndProcurement

entry.title: In Joule Skill workflows, is there any way to create a custom function to process input parameters?
entry.content:
In Joule Skill workflows, is there any way to create a custom function to process input parameters?

Currently, it seems that input parameters can only be handled as String values. If a user provides input such as “process document 1,2,3,4”, is it possible to implement a custom function to convert that String into a List of Strings and then pass it to an API?

Are there any built-in capabilities or recommended approaches to handle this kind of input transformation within Joule Skills?

entry.title: Joule - Performance and Goals Agent
entry.content:
We turned on the Joule performance and goals agent, but it is not working as expected.  I watched the agent demo video, replicated the conversation shown in the demo, and our instance is not replying the way it should be,  based on the demo conversation. Rather, it gives me a very vague reply with no actual context on my direct report's work. 

 We have all the data sources enabled, and they have data points in them, so there is context that should be generated when I ask questions. 

Does anyone have this turned on, and have you experienced any issues? I'd love to hear your feedback and any roadblocks you have run into with this agent in Joule.
